You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: ef4afbe7).

## Issue to Resolve

Drop support for ktlint-enable / ktlint-disable directive
The directives below can be used to suppress ktlint rules:
```
val foo = "foo" // ktlint-disable

/* ktlint-disable */
val foo = "foo"
/* ktlint-enable */
```

The usage of `Suppress` and `SuppressWarning` annotation however is more native and should be the only way starting from `1.0` version:
```
@Suppress("ktlint")
val foo = "foo"
```

Ideally, a rule is added which helps in autocorrecting the old directives to annotations. If this is implemented, take care that this rule itself should not be suppressed even in case for a line, block or file *all* annotations are suppressed.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

Using [Github Code Search](https://github.com/search?type=code&auto_enroll=true&q=ktlint-disable+language%3AKotlin) some insight can be collected in how the old directives are used. Unfortunately, the total number of search result is currently limited to 100 results. By excluding based on repository, some more insights can be collected but this is also limited. In summer 2023 a new search API is expected which should be able to return all search results.

Insights sofar:

If a single construct is surrounded by a block directive, it should be replaced by an annotation on that single construct:
```
        /* ktlint-disable max-line-length */
        private const val URI_REGEX = "^(?:(?![^:@]+:[^:@/]*@)([^:/?#.]+):)?(?://)?((?:(([^:@]*)(?::([^:@]*))?)?@)?([^:/?#]*)(?::(\\d*))?)(((/(?:[^?#](?![^?#/]*\\.[^?#/.]+(?:[?#]|$)))*/?)?([^?#/]*))(?:\\?([^#]*))?(?:#(.*))?)"

        /* ktlint-enable max-line-length */
```

However if the directive block contains multiple statements, it can not be simply replaced with a Suppress on the first (nor on all statements). Moving the Suppress to an outer construct is not always equivalent to current situation:
```
    @Test
    fun testParseStringForHybridFileParcelable() {
        /* ktlint-disable max-line-length */
        // ls
        val lsLines = arrayOf(
            "-rwxr-x---   1 root   shell    29431 2009-01-01 08:00 init.rc",
            "lrw-r--r--   1 root   root        15 2009-01-01 08:00 product -> /system/product",
            "drwxr-xr-x  17 root   root      4096 1970-05-19 08:40 system",
            "-r--r--r-- 1 root root 10 1970-01-13 07:32 cpu_variant:arm",
            "lrwxrwxrwx  1 root root 0 2022-10-05 15:39 ac -> ../../devices/platform/GFSH0001:00/power_supply/ac",
            "lrwxrwxrwx   1 root root 0 2022-10-05 00:16 usb -> ../../devices/platform/soc/c440000.qcom,spmi/spmi-0/spmi0-02/c440000.qcom,spmi:qcom,pm8150b@2:qcom,qpnp-smb5/power_supply/usb"
        )

        // stat with old toybox or busybox
        // val a1 = "-rwxr-x--- 1 shell root 512 2009-01-01 08:00:00.000000000 `init.rc'"
        // val b1 = "lrw-r--r-- 1 root root 512 2009-01-01 08:00:00.000000000 `product' -> `/system/product'"
        // val c1 = "drwxr-xr-x 17 root root 512 1970-05-19 08:40:27.269999949 `system'"

        // stat with new toybox
        val statLines = arrayOf(
            "-rwxr-x--- 1 shell root 512 1230796800 `init.rc'",
            "lrw-r--r-- 1 root root 512 1230796800 `product' -> `/system/product'",
            "drwxr-xr-x 17 root root 512 11922027 `system'",
            "-r--r--r-- 1 root root 512 1035141 `cpu_variant:arm'",
            "lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 512 1664955558 /sys/class/power_supply/ac -> '../../devices/platform/GFSH0001:00/power_supply/ac'",
            "lrwxrwxrwx 1 root root 512 1664956626 /sys/class/power_supply/usb -> '../../devices/platform/soc/c440000.qcom,spmi/spmi-0/spmi0-02/c440000.qcom,spmi:qcom,pm8150b@2:qcom,qpnp-smb5/power_supply/usb'"
        )
        /* ktlint-enable max-line-length */

        ....
    }
```

In example below, the block directive should be replaced with a file-annotation *or* the annotation must be interpreted as being relevant for the entire import list (but what if the annotation is not placed just before the first import?):
```
/* ktlint-disable import-ordering */
import android.app.Notification
```

In case below, the same EOL directive is repeated multiple times, but not on all lines.  Usage of the block directive would have been a better choice in this example. But this is similar to one of problems mentioned before:
```
  val result = CharArray(16)
  result[ 0] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 60 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 1] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 56 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 2] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 52 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 3] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 48 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 4] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 44 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 5] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 40 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 6] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 36 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 7] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 32 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 8] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 28 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[ 9] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 24 and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[10] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 20 and 0xf).toInt()]
  result[11] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 16 and 0xf).toInt()]
  result[12] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 12 and 0xf).toInt()]
  result[13] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 8  and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[14] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this shr 4  and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
  result[15] = HEX_DIGIT_CHARS[(this        and 0xf).toInt()] // ktlint-disable no-multi-spaces
```

References to old `experimental` rule set should be replaced with `standard`:
```
        /* ktlint-disable experimental:argument-list-wrapping */
        fixture.getSut(replacements = mapOf(Replacement.FileInputStream.STRING))
```

In example below, the block directive should be replace with a file annotation:
```
/* ktlint-disable */
@file:Suppress("PackageName", "TopLevelName")
```

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `.editorconfig`
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/faq.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/experimental.md`
- `documentation/snapshot/docs/rules/standard.md`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/build.gradle.kts`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/api/KtLintRuleEngine.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleExecutionContext.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/RuleProviderSorter.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/SuppressionLocatorBuilder.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rulefilter/InternalRuleProvidersFilter.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rulefilter/RuleFilter.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/InternalRule.kt`
- `ktlint-rule-engine/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/rule/engine/internal/rules/KtlintSuppressionRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/MaxLineLengthRule.kt`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

217â†’            ktAnnotated
218â†’                .annotationEntries
219â†’                .filter {
220â†’                    it.calleeExpression
221â†’                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222â†’                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223â†’                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224â†’                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }

226â†’                    when {
227â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229â†’                            SuppressionHint(

231â†’                                emptySet(),
232â†’                            )

234â†’                        else ->

238â†’                            )

243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244â†’        getArgumentExpression()

246â†’            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")

248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252â†’                    }
253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")

266â†’    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =

269â†’            // not prefixed with any rule set id.

276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an

1â†’## Why should I use ktlint?
  2â†’
  3â†’the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4â†’
  5â†’Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6â†’
  7â†’By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8â†’
  9â†’ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10â†’
 11â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12â†’
 13â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14â†’```editorconfig
 15â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18â†’```
 19â†’
 20â†’!!! note
 21â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22â†’
 23â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24â†’
 25â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26â†’```editorconfig
 27â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30â†’```
 31â†’
 32â†’!!! note
 33â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34â†’
 35â†’## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36â†’
 37â†’Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38â†’
 39â†’See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40â†’
 41â†’## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42â†’
 43â†’!!! tip
 44â†’    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45â†’
 46â†’To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47â†’
 48â†’An error can be suppressed using:
 49â†’
 50â†’* EOL comments
 51â†’* Block comments
 52â†’* @Suppress annotations
 53â†’
 54â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55â†’
 56â†’    ```kotlin
 57â†’    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58â†’    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59â†’
 60â†’    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62â†’    class Foo {}
 63â†’
 64â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66â†’    import foo.*
 67â†’
 68â†’    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69â†’    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70â†’    import foo.*
 71â†’    ```
 72â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73â†’
 74â†’    ```kotlin
 75â†’    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77â†’
 78â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80â†’
 81â†’    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83â†’    ```
 84â†’
 85â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86â†’
 87â†’    ```kotlin
 88â†’    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90â†’    import foo.*
 91â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92â†’
 93â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95â†’    import foo.*
 96â†’    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97â†’
 98â†’    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99â†’    /* ktlint-disable */
100â†’    import foo.*
101â†’    /* ktlint-enable */
102â†’    ```
103â†’
104â†’!!! important
105â†’    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106â†’
107â†’!!! warning
108â†’    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109â†’
110â†’## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111â†’
112â†’When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113â†’
114â†’
115â†’## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116â†’
117â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118â†’
119â†’A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120â†’
121â†’Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122â†’```editorconfig
123â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129â†’```
130â†’
131â†’!!! note
132â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133â†’
134â†’!!! note
135â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136â†’
137â†’## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138â†’
139â†’The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140â†’
141â†’## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142â†’
143â†’Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144â†’
145â†’Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146â†’
147â†’Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148â†’
149â†’!!! tip
150â†’    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151â†’
152â†’## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153â†’
154â†’Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155â†’To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156â†’
157â†’!!! warning
158â†’    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159â†’
160â†’To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161â†’
162â†’```kotlin
163â†’val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164â†’  ServiceLoader
165â†’      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166â†’      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167â†’}
168â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169â†’  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170â†’  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171â†’)
172â†’ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173â†’```
174â†’Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175â†’
176â†’It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177â†’and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178â†’```kotlin
179â†’kotlinFile.writeText(
180â†’  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181â†’    Code.CodeSnippet(
182â†’      stringBuilder.toString()
183â†’    )
184â†’  )
185â†’)
186â†’```
187â†’
188â†’# Are formatter tags respected?
189â†’
190â†’As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191â†’```editorconfig
192â†’ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193â†’ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194â†’ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195â†’```
196â†’
197â†’When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.
198â†’

Here's a precise plan for dropping support for `ktlint-disable` / `ktlint-enable` directives in comments, and shifting exclusively to annotation-based suppression as described:

## Plan

1. **SuppressionLocatorBuilder**
   - Remove all parsing and detection of comment-based suppressions (`// ktlint-disable`, `/* ktlint-disable */`, `/* ktlint-enable */`), including `CommentSuppressionHint`, its usages, and references to these constants.

2. **MaxLineLengthRule & NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule**
   - Remove handling and logic that refers to `ktlint-disable` comments, including comment detection exceptions and related notes.

3. **Documentation**
   - Remove references to comment-based suppression from code comments in rules.
   - Ensure documentation refers only to annotation-based suppression (`@Suppress("ktlint")` or per-rule suppression).

4. **Changelog**
   - The changelog appears to already declare the removal, so ensure comments and references are up to date, but do not update it further unless found necessary.

5. **No-op for code that only refers to the annotation approach** â€“ do not touch code that does not reference comment-based suppression.

## Implementation

I'll begin by editing SuppressionLocatorBuilder to remove all comment-based suppression handling, and then remove related logic in affected rules.

 21â†’
 22â†’/**
 23â†’ * Detects if given `ruleId` at given `offset` is suppressed.
 24â†’ */
 25â†’internal typealias SuppressionLocator = (offset: Int, ruleId: RuleId) -> Boolean
 26â†’
 27â†’internal object SuppressionLocatorBuilder {
 28â†’    /**
 29â†’     * No suppression is detected. Always returns `false`.
 30â†’     */
 31â†’    private val NO_SUPPRESSION: SuppressionLocator = { _, _ -> false }
 32â†’
 33â†’    private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 34â†’
 35â†’    /**
 36â†’     * Mapping of non-ktlint annotations to ktlint-annotation so that ktlint rules will be suppressed automatically
 37â†’     * when specific non-ktlint annotations are found. The prevents that developers have to specify multiple annotations
 38â†’     * for the same violation.
 39â†’     */
 40â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP =
 41â†’        mapOf(
 42â†’            // It would have been nice if the official rule id's as defined in the Rules themselves could have been used here. But that would
 43â†’            // introduce a circular dependency between the ktlint-rule-engine and the ktlint-ruleset-standard modules.
 44â†’            "EnumEntryName" to RuleId("standard:enum-entry-name-case"),
 45â†’            "RemoveCurlyBracesFromTemplate" to RuleId("standard:string-template"),
 46â†’            "ClassName" to RuleId("standard:class-naming"),
 47â†’            "FunctionName" to RuleId("standard:function-naming"),
 48â†’            "PackageName" to RuleId("standard:package-name"),
 49â†’            "PropertyName" to RuleId("standard:property-naming"),
 50â†’        )
 51â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS = setOf("Suppress", "SuppressWarnings")
 52â†’    private val SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID = RuleId("ktlint:suppress-all-rules")
 53â†’
 54â†’    /**
 55â†’     * Builds [SuppressionLocator] for given [rootNode] of AST tree.
 56â†’     */
 57â†’    fun buildSuppressedRegionsLocator(
 58â†’        rootNode: ASTNode,
 59â†’        editorConfig: EditorConfig,
 60â†’    ): SuppressionLocator {
 61â†’        val hintsList = collect(rootNode, FormatterTags.from(editorConfig))
 62â†’        return if (hintsList.isEmpty()) {
 63â†’            NO_SUPPRESSION
 64â†’        } else {
 65â†’            toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList)
 66â†’        }
 67â†’    }
 68â†’
 69â†’    private fun toSuppressedRegionsLocator(hintsList: List<SuppressionHint>): SuppressionLocator =
 70â†’        { offset, ruleId ->
 71â†’            hintsList
 72â†’                .filter { offset in it.range }
 73â†’                .any { hint -> hint.disabledRuleIds.isEmpty() || hint.disabledRuleIds.contains(ruleId) }
 74â†’        }
 75â†’

243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =

248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID

253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257â†’                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }

259â†’                    else -> {

276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an

278â†’            LOGGER.warn {
279â†’                """
280â†’                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281â†’                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}

21â†’/**
22â†’ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23â†’ */
24â†’public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25â†’    StandardRule(
26â†’        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28â†’            setOf(
29â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31â†’            ),
32â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33â†’    ),
34â†’    Rule.Experimental,
35â†’    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37â†’        node: ASTNode,
38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40â†’    ) {
41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42â†’            val afterBlockComment =
43â†’                node
44â†’                    .leaves()
45â†’                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46â†’                    .firstOrNull()
47â†’                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48â†’
49â†’            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50â†’                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51â†’                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52â†’            ) {
53â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
55â†’                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56â†’                }
57â†’            }
58â†’        }
59â†’    }
60â†’
61â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65â†’        rawRemove()
66â†’    }
67â†’
68â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
69â†’
70â†’    // TODO: Remove when ktlint suppression directive in comments are no longer supported
71â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() =
72â†’        this
73â†’            ?.text
74â†’            ?.removePrefix("/*")
75â†’            ?.removeSuffix("*/")
76â†’            ?.trim()
77â†’            ?.let { it.startsWith("ktlint-enable") || it.startsWith("ktlint-disable") }
78â†’            ?: false
79â†’}

243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244â†’        getArgumentExpression()
245â†’            ?.text
246â†’            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247â†’            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252â†’                    }
253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257â†’                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258â†’                    }
259â†’                    else -> {
260â†’                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261â†’                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]

266â†’    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267â†’        try {
268â†’            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269â†’            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270â†’            RuleId
271â†’                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272â†’                .let { RuleId(it) }
273â†’        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274â†’            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275â†’            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277â†’            // unqualified ruleId.
278â†’            LOGGER.warn {
279â†’                """
280â†’                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281â†’                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282â†’                """.trimIndent()
283â†’            }
284â†’            null
285â†’        }

1â†’## Why should I use ktlint?
  2â†’
  3â†’the short answer is **Simplicity**.
  4â†’
  5â†’Spending time on configuration (and maintenance down the road) of hundred-line long style config file(s) is counter-productive. Instead of wasting your energy on something that has no business value - focus on what really matters (not debating whether to use tabs or spaces).
  6â†’
  7â†’By using ktlint you put the importance of code clarity and community conventions over personal preferences. This makes things easier for people reading your code as well as frees you from having to document and explain what style potential contributor(s) have to follow.
  8â†’
  9â†’ktlint is a single binary with both linter & formatter included. All you need is to drop it in (no need to get [overwhelmed](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Decision_fatigue) while choosing among [dozens of code style options](https://checkstyle.sourceforge.net/checks.html)).
 10â†’
 11â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule?
 12â†’
 13â†’An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 14â†’```editorconfig
 15â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 16â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 17â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 18â†’```
 19â†’
 20â†’!!! note
 21â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 22â†’
 23â†’##  How do I enable or disable a rule set?
 24â†’
 25â†’All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 26â†’```editorconfig
 27â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 28â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 29â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 30â†’```
 31â†’
 32â†’!!! note
 33â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
 34â†’
 35â†’## Can I have my own rules on top of ktlint?
 36â†’
 37â†’Absolutely, "no configuration" doesn't mean "no extensibility". You can add your own ruleset(s) to discover potential bugs, check for anti-patterns, etc.
 38â†’
 39â†’See [adding a custom rule set](../api/custom-rule-set/) for more information.
 40â†’
 41â†’## How do I suppress errors for a line/block/file?
 42â†’
 43â†’!!! tip
 44â†’    Suppressing a `ktlint` violation is meant primarily as an escape latch for the rare cases when **ktlint** is not able to produce the correct result. Please report any such instances using [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues)).
 45â†’
 46â†’To disable a specific rule you'll need the rule identifier which is displayed at the end of the lint error.
 47â†’
 48â†’An error can be suppressed using:
 49â†’
 50â†’* EOL comments
 51â†’* Block comments
 52â†’* @Suppress annotations
 53â†’
 54â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) Suppress annotation"
 55â†’
 56â†’    ```kotlin
 57â†’    // Suppressing all rules for the entire file
 58â†’    @file:Suppress("ktlint")
 59â†’
 60â†’    // Suppress a single rule (with id 'rule-id', defined in rule set with id 'rule-set-id') from the annotated construct
 61â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:rule-set-id:rule-id")
 62â†’    class Foo {}
 63â†’
 64â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the annotated construct
 65â†’    @Suppress("ktlint:standard:no-wildcard-imports", "ktlint:custom-rule-set-id:custom-rule-id")
 66â†’    import foo.*
 67â†’
 68â†’    // Suppress all rules for the annotated construct
 69â†’    @Suppress("ktlint")
 70â†’    import foo.*
 71â†’    ```
 72â†’=== "[:material-heart:](#) EOL comments"
 73â†’
 74â†’    ```kotlin
 75â†’    // Suppress a single rule for the commented line
 76â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports
 77â†’
 78â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for the commented line
 79â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_other-rule-id
 80â†’
 81â†’    // Suppress all rules for the commented line
 82â†’    import foo.* // ktlint-disable
 83â†’    ```
 84â†’
 85â†’=== "[:material-heart-off-outline:](#) Block comments"
 86â†’
 87â†’    ```kotlin
 88â†’    // Suppress a single rule for all code between the start and end tag
 89â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 90â†’    import foo.*
 91â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 92â†’
 93â†’    // Suppress multiple rules for all code between the start and end tag
 94â†’    /* ktlint-disable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 95â†’    import foo.*
 96â†’    /* ktlint-enable standard_no-wildcard-imports standard_no-wildcard-imports */
 97â†’
 98â†’    // Suppress all rules for all code between the start and end tag
 99â†’    /* ktlint-disable */
100â†’    import foo.*
101â†’    /* ktlint-enable */
102â†’    ```
103â†’
104â†’!!! important
105â†’    When using the block comments, the `ktlint-enable` directive needs to specify the exact same rule-id's and in the same order as the `ktlint-disable` directive.
106â†’
107â†’!!! warning
108â†’    From a consistency perspective seen, it might be best to **not** mix the (EOL/Block) comment style with the annotation style in the same project.
109â†’
110â†’## How do I globally disable a rule without `.editorconfig`?
111â†’
112â†’When using Ktlint CLI, you may pass a list of disabled rules via the `--disabled_rules` command line flag. The value is a comma separated list of rule id's that have to be disabled. The rule id must be fully qualified (e.g. must be prefixed with the rule set id). 
113â†’
114â†’
115â†’## Why is `.editorconfig` property `disabled_rules` deprecated and how do I resolve this?
116â†’
117â†’The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are deprecated as of KtLint version `0.48` and are removed in version `0.49`. Those properties contain a comma separated list of rules which are disabled. Using a comma separated list of values has some disadvantages.
118â†’
119â†’A big disadvantage is that it is not possible to override the property partially in an `.editorconfig` file in a subpackage. Another disadvantage is that it is not possible to express explicitly that a rule is enabled. Lastly, (qualified) rule ids can be 20 characters or longer, which makes a list with multiple entries hard to read.
120â†’
121â†’Starting with KtLint `0.48` entire rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set). Examples:
122â†’```editorconfig
123â†’ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
124â†’ktlint_standard_final-newline = enabled # Enables the `final-newline` rule in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
125â†’ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
126â†’ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = disabled # Disables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
127â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
128â†’ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
129â†’```
130â†’
131â†’!!! note
132â†’    All rules from the `standard` and custom rule sets are *enabled* by default and can optionally be disabled in the `.editorconfig`. All `experimental` rules are *disabled* by default and can optionally be enabled in the `.editorconfig`.
133â†’
134â†’!!! note
135â†’    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.  
136â†’
137â†’## Why is wildcard import `java.util.*` not reported by the `no-wildcard-imports` rule?
138â†’
139â†’The `no-wildcard-imports` rule forbids wildcard imports, except for imports defined in `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand`. If this property is not explicitly set, it allows wildcards imports like `java.util.*` by default to keep in sync with IntelliJ IDEA behavior.
140â†’
141â†’## Can a new toggle be added to optionally (enable/disable) format code in a particular way?
142â†’
143â†’Ktlint can be configured by enabling and disabling rules. Some rules can be configured in more details with additional `.editorconfig` properties. Regularly, a new configuration option is requested to modify behavior in existing rules.
144â†’
145â†’Ktlint is restrictive with adding additional configuration settings to customize behavior in rules. Each configuration option that Ktlint offers comes with complexity that has to be maintained by only a couple of maintainers. As of that, we cannot provide tens or even hundreds of such options.
146â†’
147â†’Less configuration options also means less discussions in teams about settings to use. Unfortunately this means that you cannot tweak Ktlint exactly to the format you prefer.
148â†’
149â†’!!! tip
150â†’    Any idea for a new configuration option is valuable. Please create an issue for it so that it can be considered to incorporate it in Ktlint.
151â†’
152â†’## Can I use KtLint to directly format the code I'm generating with KotlinPoet?
153â†’
154â†’Yes, it is possible to use KtLint to directly format the code generated with KotlinPoet. 
155â†’To do so, you must include the dependencies `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-core` and `com.pinterest.ktlint:ktlint-ruleset-standard` in your Gradle/Maven project.
156â†’
157â†’!!! warning
158â†’    Do not include the dependency `com.pinterest:ktlint` as that would import the entire ktlint project including unwanted dependencies. Besides a much bigger artifact, it might also result in problems regarding logging.
159â†’
160â†’To format the output of KotlinPoet with KtLint, you can use the following snippet:
161â†’
162â†’```kotlin
163â†’val ruleProviders = buildSet {
164â†’  ServiceLoader
165â†’      .load(RuleSetProviderV2::class.java)
166â†’      .flatMapTo(this) { it.getRuleProviders() }
167â†’}
168â†’val ktLintRuleEngine = KtLintRuleEngine(
169â†’  ruleProviders = ruleProviders,
170â†’  editorConfigDefaults = EditorConfigDefaults.load(EDITORCONFIG_PATH),
171â†’)
172â†’ktLintRuleEngine.format(outputDir.toPath())
173â†’```
174â†’Here, outputDir refers to the directory of the generated files by KotlinPoet, ktLintRuleEngine is an instance of KtLint rule engine.
175â†’
176â†’It is also possible to format file-by-file the output of KotlinPoet if you write your `FileSpec` to a `StringBuilder()`, instead of a `File`, 
177â†’and send the generated code as `String` to KtLint inside a `CodeSnippet`:
178â†’```kotlin
179â†’kotlinFile.writeText(
180â†’  ktLintRuleEngine.format(
181â†’    Code.CodeSnippet(
182â†’      stringBuilder.toString()
183â†’    )
184â†’  )
185â†’)
186â†’```
187â†’
188â†’# Are formatter tags respected?
189â†’
190â†’As of version `0.49.x` the formatter tags of IntelliJ IDEA are respected. By default, those formatter tags are disabled. The formatter tags can be enabled with `.editorconfig` properties below:
191â†’```editorconfig
192â†’ij_formatter_tags_enabled = true # Defaults to 'false'
193â†’ij_formatter_off_tag = some-custom-off-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:off'
194â†’ij_formatter_on_tag = some-custom-on-tag # Defaults to '@formatter:on'
195â†’```
196â†’
197â†’When enabled, the ktlint rule checking is disabled for all code surrounded by the formatter tags.

211â†’    /**
212â†’     * Creates [SuppressionHint] from annotations of given [PsiElement]. Returns null if no targetAnnotations are
213â†’     * present or no mapping exists between annotations' values and ktlint rules
214â†’     */
215â†’    private fun PsiElement.createSuppressionHintFromAnnotations(): SuppressionHint? =
216â†’        (this as? KtAnnotated)?.let { ktAnnotated ->
217â†’            ktAnnotated
218â†’                .annotationEntries
219â†’                .filter {
220â†’                    it.calleeExpression
221â†’                        ?.constructorReferenceExpression
222â†’                        ?.getReferencedName() in SUPPRESS_ANNOTATIONS
223â†’                }.flatMap(KtAnnotationEntry::getValueArguments)
224â†’                .mapNotNull { it.toRuleId(SUPPRESS_ANNOTATION_RULE_MAP) }
225â†’                .let { suppressedRuleIds ->
226â†’                    when {
227â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.isEmpty() -> null
228â†’                        suppressedRuleIds.contains(SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID) ->
229â†’                            SuppressionHint(
230â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
231â†’                                emptySet(),
232â†’                            )
233â†’
234â†’                        else ->
235â†’                            SuppressionHint(
236â†’                                IntRange(ktAnnotated.startOffset, ktAnnotated.endOffset),
237â†’                                suppressedRuleIds.toSet(),
238â†’                            )
239â†’                    }
240â†’                }
241â†’        }

243â†’    private fun ValueArgument.toRuleId(annotationValueToRuleMapping: Map<String, RuleId>): RuleId? =
244â†’        getArgumentExpression()
245â†’            ?.text
246â†’            ?.removeSurrounding("\"")
247â†’            ?.let { argumentExpressionText ->
248â†’                when {
249â†’                    argumentExpressionText == "ktlint" -> {
250â†’                        // Disable all rules
251â†’                        SUPPRESS_ALL_KTLINT_RULES_RULE_ID
252â†’                    }
253â†’                    argumentExpressionText.startsWith("ktlint:") -> {
254â†’                        // Disable specific rule
255â†’                        argumentExpressionText
256â†’                            .removePrefix("ktlint:")
257â†’                            .let { createRuleIdOrNull(it) }
258â†’                    }
259â†’                    else -> {
260â†’                        // Disable specific rule if the annotation value is mapped to a specific rule
261â†’                        annotationValueToRuleMapping[argumentExpressionText]
262â†’                    }
263â†’                }
264â†’            }

266â†’    private fun createRuleIdOrNull(ruleId: String): RuleId? =
267â†’        try {
268â†’            // For backwards compatibility the suppression hints have to be prefixed with the standard rule set id when the rule id is
269â†’            // not prefixed with any rule set id.
270â†’            RuleId
271â†’                .prefixWithStandardRuleSetIdWhenMissing(ruleId)
272â†’                .let { RuleId(it) }
273â†’        } catch (illegalArgumentException: IllegalArgumentException) {
274â†’            // Ktlint should not terminate with an exception in case the code being scanned contains a suppression for a non-existing rule.
275â†’            // Instead, a warning should be printed and the invalid reference is to be ignored. The original ruleId is printed in the
276â†’            // warning message so that user will not go searching for the fully qualified rule id while the code actually contained an
277â†’            // unqualified ruleId.
278â†’            LOGGER.warn {
279â†’                """
280â†’                Can not suppress rule with id '$ruleId'. Please check and fix references to this rule in your code.
281â†’                    Underlying cause: ${illegalArgumentException.message}
282â†’                """.trimIndent()
283â†’            }
284â†’            null
285â†’        }

291â†’    private data class SuppressionHint(

21â†’/**
22â†’ * A block comment following another element on the same line is replaced with an EOL comment, if possible.
23â†’ */
24â†’public class NoSingleLineBlockCommentRule :
25â†’    StandardRule(
26â†’        id = "no-single-line-block-comment",
27â†’        usesEditorConfigProperties =
28â†’            setOf(
29â†’                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
30â†’                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
31â†’            ),
32â†’        visitorModifiers = setOf(RunAfterRule(COMMENT_WRAPPING_RULE_ID, REGARDLESS_WHETHER_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_OR_DISABLED)),
33â†’    ),
34â†’    Rule.Experimental,
35â†’    Rule.OfficialCodeStyle {
36â†’    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
37â†’        node: ASTNode,
38â†’        autoCorrect: Boolean,
39â†’        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
40â†’    ) {
41â†’        if (node.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT) {
42â†’            val afterBlockComment =
43â†’                node
44â†’                    .leaves()
45â†’                    .takeWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() && !it.textContains('\n') }
46â†’                    .firstOrNull()
47â†’                    ?: node.lastChildLeafOrSelf()
48â†’
49â†’            if (!node.textContains('\n') &&
50â†’                !node.isKtlintSuppressionDirective() &&
51â†’                afterBlockComment.nextLeaf().isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull()
52â†’            ) {
53â†’                emit(node.startOffset, "Replace the block comment with an EOL comment", true)
54â†’                if (autoCorrect) {
55â†’                    node.replaceWithEndOfLineComment()
56â†’                }
57â†’            }
58â†’        }
59â†’    }

61â†’    private fun ASTNode.replaceWithEndOfLineComment() {
62â†’        val content = text.removeSurrounding("/*", "*/").trim()
63â†’        val eolComment = PsiCommentImpl(EOL_COMMENT, "// $content")
64â†’        (this as LeafPsiElement).rawInsertBeforeMe(eolComment)
65â†’        rawRemove()
66â†’    }

68â†’    private fun ASTNode?.isWhitespaceWithNewlineOrNull() = this == null || this.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()
